slides.title: PPT Generator
slides.subtitle: Turn an idea into a finished, editable deck — in any visual style
slides.author: Built with Claude Code

slides.title: Separate what you say from how it looks
body: You write the content once as a structured spec — what goes on each slide and which layout carries it. A renderer turns that into a native PowerPoint file.
Because content and style are separate, the same deck can be re-rendered in any template by changing a single field. No design work repeated, no copy-paste reformatting.

slides.title: Five steps from idea to file
slides.lead: The skill does the content design, not just text dumping.

bullets.text: Understand the topic, audience, and length
Pick a template that matches the tone
Design slides as a JSON spec
Choosing the right layout per slide is the craft
Verify and iterate — restyle or refine in seconds

slides.title: Pick a look to fit the room

slides.quote: Slides support the speaker — they aren't the document. One idea per slide, lead with the point.

slides.title: Inspired by Presenton, runs fully local
body: The open-source Presenton project pioneered the pipeline: AI-generated content feeds a structured layout, which a renderer turns into the final file.
This skill keeps that pipeline but drops the server — it produces real, editable PowerPoint directly through python-pptx, on your own machine, with no API or service required.

slides.title: Make a deck
slides.subtitle: Just describe the topic — the skill handles the rest.
